observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
The Liquidating Officer, to verify the Report of Collections and Deposits; check the statement of accountable forms as to initial balances on hand, receipt, issued, and ending balances on hand; make a physical count of the accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collector/teller and check the same against the new balances on hand column. Also, require her to verify the ORs by affixing a signature at the back of the triplicate copy of the last official receipt issued.  She shall count the money turned over to her and sign the certification and receipt portion of all copies of RCD, at the end of the day;
The Municipal Treasurer to maintain subsidiary records for each deputized collector supported with files of the RCDs submitted to facilitate easy retrieval of records for validation and verification purposes; and
The Property Custodian of the AFs to prepare the Statement of Accountability of Accountable Forms with or without monetary value on a monthly basis, duly supported with the requisitioned issue slip by each accountable officer and a copy of the RCD to give an idea as to the serial numbers and the quantity reported on hand.
The Municipal Treasurer together with the Liquidating Officer who at the same time the Property Custodian of the Accountable Forms committed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct or direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately close the account with Metro Bank-Catarman Branch and transfer the balance to the General Fund and present the documents for audit.
We further recommended that opening a depository account with private bank should be avoided, unless any of the circumstances cited is present and the requirements required in BLGF DC 01-2017 is complied with.
The Municipal Treasurer committed to comply with the recommendations. The withdrawal slip of the amount of ₱68,820.02 and subsequently the deposit to the LGU account of the same amount was presented during the audit exit conference.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to properly recognize completed infrastructure projects to its appropriate asset account and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the accurate balances of the property, plant, and equipment accounts in the financial statements.
The Municipal Accountant asserted that the non-reclassification of some completed projects to the appropriate PPE accounts was due to the unpaid  final billing pending submission of the final billing statements by the contractor. Nevertheless, she committed to comply with the recommendation and  will make the necessary adjustments.
The Audit Team suggested that coordination between the Municipal Engineering Office and Municipal Accounting Office as regards to the implementation of Infra Structure Projects is concerned is necessary so that the latter will be abreast of the status of the projects and that the proper timing of recording in the books of accounts could be done.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the HRMO specifically the Payroll Officer to submit any changes in the payroll to the GSIS every month so that the GSIS system can capture the updated data, hence, accurate computation of the premiums, can be arrived and reflected in the billing statements and the corresponding payroll deductions and remittances of the same could be made accurately.

The Municipal Accountant asserted that the abnormal balance usually happened when there were newly hired and promoted employees where remittances were made ahead of the withholding of the same amount to comply within the reglementary period, since the GSIS system will not accept the remittances if it is not equal to the billed amount. The related payroll deduction is made on the following month where the remittances apply.

Further, the Internal Auditor stated that she already informed the GSIS about the changes but the GSIS failed to effect the updated data.

The Audit Team suggested that a follow up or a visit to the GSIS office  Billing and Collection Section to have a reconciliation of records be done immediately.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to require the:
Municipal Accountant to maintain a subsidiary ledger per project in order to monitor the balances of the 15% advance payment as well as the recoupment thereto and ensure that all transactions be captured and posted to the general ledgers so that accurate balances be reported;
The Project Engineers concerned to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and the Project Monitoring Committee and verify the projects with unrecouped advances; and communicate to the contractors of possible application of unrecoup amount from any unpaid claims and from other projects undertaken by the same contractors; and, otherwise initiate legal action against those who will refuse to refund the unrecouped advances, if warranted.
The Municipal Accountant alleged that she has already complied with the recommendation. The subsidiary ledgers per project was presented during the exit conference.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accounting Office to provide the actual net pay of the employees when the loan application is filed and make the Due to GSIS account priority over and above other deductions in the payroll.
Require also the HRMO/Payroll Officer to submit any changes in the payroll to the GSIS every month so that the GSIS system can capture the updated data hence, accurate computation of the premiums, and loans obtained can be established and reflected in the billing statements and the corresponding payroll deductions and remittances of the same could be made accurately.
Furthermore, we recommended that the above employees settle their obligations to the LGU immediately and the Municipal Accounting Office to monitor the refunds and submit progress report every month.

The Municipal Accountant averred that she already sent demand letters last December 2024 to the above listed LGU personnel. She then added that the LGU should have not paid on behalf of the loans of its employees. However, the billing of the GSIS is system-generated, thus the accounting office is forced to pay for all rather than compromise the good payors. Nonetheless, some of the employees acknowledged and committed on paying their loan. One of the defaulting employees present reasoned out that he already paid  the amount due him.

The Audit Team Leader emphasized that in case the loan amortization is not deducted in the payroll the debtor-employee should pay his/her obligations through an over the counter scheme in the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to satisfy their obligations, which in effect replenishes the LGU funds. She further stated that GSIS deductions should be prioritized over and above other loan deductions from private institutions or organizations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Treasurer and other officials concerned to adequately insure all its insurable properties, with the GIF of GSIS as protection against losses or damages. Likewise, the same offices should collaborate with GSO to spearhead the undertaking in identifying other serviceable and insurable properties and in the preparation of Inventory of all insurable properties for submission to the GIF of the GSIS.
The General Services Officer and Municipal Treasurer committed to comply with the recommendation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the LDRRM Fund be utilized solely for disaster risk management activities, and properly charge all disbursements to its appropriate PPA.

The Municipal Budget Officer averred that it lacked the needed funds to implement the PPA, which is why various disbursements were charged therein that have no direct relation to the same. He added that the charging of expenditures should be the sole responsibility of each office, and the Municipal Budget Office’s role should fall squarely only with certifying that the disbursement is supported with an approved appropriation. He then averred that, currently, the LDRRM Office have implemented the same because they are acquainted with the expenditures under their office. Further, he reasoned that in the CAFOA, there is a specific portion pertaining to the requesting office where it provided for the details where a disbursement would be charged. Nonetheless, such observation is noted and stated that the improper charging may only be due to clerical errors.

The Audit Team Leader reminded the Municipal Budget Officer that what was programmed should have been disbursed for expenditures that is directly related to the PPA, instead of charging the same to various disbursements not related thereto. On the other hand, she asserted that such practice of passing the responsibility to all offices in determining the appropriate charges of their expenditures is not appropriate since that is the job of a Municipal Budget Officer, he should have the final say. Despite a provision in the CAFOA of a portion where an expense is to be charged and signed by the requesting office, it never changes his responsibility since he is the proper official to determine the appropriate charges to the budget.  She emphasized that it is the requesting office have filled-in where the expenses to be charged, still, it is the task of the Municipal Budget Officer to ascertain the validity of the charges.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, require the HRMO to coordinate with Sangguniang Bayan Members in order to come up with concrete policy guidelines concerning the hiring and rehiring of COS and JO personnel to establish a “clear expectation”.
We further recommended that upon hiring of COS/JO personnel, they should be provided with a Statement of Duties and Responsibilities to clearly identify their responsibilities and perform their duties efficiently.
Also, we recommended that the human resources division should set a standardized wage rate based on their qualifications and suitability for the job applied for.
The HRMO committed to comply with the recommendation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Person to strictly observe the timelines in the preparation, submission and posting of the GAD Accomplishment Report, as provided by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01, dated January 12, 2016, to give the AT ample time to determine the judicious utilization thereof and whether gender issues are appropriately addressed.

The GAD Focal Person committed to comply with the recommendation.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor, together with the rest of the members of the Local School Board, the following courses of action:
Ensure that the date of provision of a copy of the SEF Budget to the Local Budget Officer, Local Accountant and Local Treasurer be appropriately logged;
Direct the prompt submission of quarterly and annual reports to the DepEd CO, copy furnished the local sanggunian concerned and regional offices of the DBM and DILG within the timeline; and
Monitor the posting of utilization report of SEF budget, pursuant to the Full Disclosure Policy of the DILG.
The Local School Board committed to comply with the recommendation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive take the following courses of action:
Direct the BAC Chairperson to ensure that the 25% of the monthly basic salary ceiling is imposed in the computation of honorarium of the BAC members that is based on the number of completed procurement projects in order to avoid any overpayment;
Enjoin the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly prohibit any non-member of the BAC, TWG or BAC Secretariat and those who are members of the latter whose regular position is in the procurement unit of the LGU from availing such form of compensation; and
Stop granting Honoraria to those who did not personally attend to the conduct of BAC meetings or proceedings.
The BAC committed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

Send written notices to the payees to verify the existence of the stale checks. A replacement check may be issued upon submission of the stale check;

Practice sending written notice to the payee at least one month before a check becomes stale; and

Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the necessary adjustment/s in the books of accounts, if any.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:

Refrain or limit the practice of serving meals and snacks during meetings, particularly those instances which are considered regular or normal activities of the Municipality, and exercise prudence in utilizing the funds which are intended for public purposes; henceforth ensure the submission of documents necessary to support the validity and propriety of the meal expenses;

Review the expenditures for catering services and ensure that the same directly supports the objective of the LGU; and

Meticulously and judiciously plan the procurement process and consider the period of implementation embodied in the PPMP of each office. Also, instruct the BAC and its members to adhere strictly to the Revised IRR of RA 9184, particularly Sections 48.2, 52.1, and 54 regarding the conduct of public bidding.
observations.managementAction:
Austerity measures are practiced in the serving of meals or snacks during regular meetings or activities by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to secure the MOA between the LGU and the NGO beneficiaries and henceforth ensure that programs to be implemented in partnership with various organizations should be covered by a Memorandum of Agreement, duly signed by the parties, so as to assure compliance with the terms and conditions laid down in achieving the goal of the program.

We recommend that Management strictly observe the relevant sections of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, and the Documentary requirements outlined in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, in the grant, utilization, and replenishment of the Petty Cash Fund. from the execution/issuance of the Contract/Purchase Order.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and Municipal
Engineer, in coordination with other officials concerned, to ensure that
at the planning stage, every reasonable effort is exerted to determine and
address any issue that may affect the implementation of the projects so
as to ensure the timely completion of the Infrastructure Projects funded
out of the 20% DF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office to optimize the use of
its DRRM funds taking into consideration all the thematic areas as
mandated under Republic Act No. 10121 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG
JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommended that the: a. Local Chief Executive direct the designated Supply Officer to conduct physical inventory, prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI), pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGU; and b. Management strictly comply with Sections 119, 121 and 123 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I on records to be maintained and proper documentation in the issuances of supplies using the prescribed forms.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is in the process of preparation of Stock in process, while Conduct of Physical Inventory done every year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. Require the General Services Officer (GSO) and the Disposal Committee to prepare the corresponding Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) for the identified unserviceable properties and initiate the process of disposing the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445 and the rules provided in COA Circular No. 92-386; and b. After the disposal, require the GSO to submit the necessary documents, together with the official receipt to the Accounting Office, as the basis of the latter to drop the unserviceable properties from the books of accounts in accordance with the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Unserviceable Properties is still on going

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Engineer Office: Provide the complete segregation / description of road projects, as well as the report on the repair/maintenance of each road network; The General Services Officer/Inventory Officer: Prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network (RPCLRN); Maintain Local Road Network Property Cards for each roads and road components; The Municipal Accountant: Adhere to the accounting policy on non-inclusion of residual value in the computation of the depreciation expense for the depreciable component of the Local Road Network; Prepare the adjusting entry to correct the understatement of Depreciation Expense to correct the error committed; Maintain subsidiary records for road and road components for every road network.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant initiated the cost accounts. The General Services Officer/Inventory Officer: Prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network (RPCLRN); Maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards for each roads and road components. The Municipal Accountant: Adhere to the accounting policy on non-inclusion of residual value in the computation of the depreciation expense for the depreciable component of the Local Road Network; Prepare the adjusting entry to correct the understatement of Depreciation Expense to correct the error committed; Maintain subsidiary records for road and road components for every road network.
observations.reason: The Mun. Engineer was not able to provide the complete description and cost component due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Local Chief Executive: 1.) To utilize the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Funds that shall address the four thematic areas of the LDRRM pursuant to the Joint Memorandum Circular. Likewise, to instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to charge to the Fund only disaster related expenditures. While the maintenance and other operating expenditures, and capital outlay of the LDRRM Office should be charged to the General Fund. 2.) To instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Officer to propose and recommend programs to be included in the AIP that are allowed under the Circular. 3.) To instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to correct the erroneous classification of the motorcycle as Motor Vehicle instead of Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment in the financial statements. 4.) To stop incurring unnecessary expenditures.
observations.reason: Due to poor planning, salaries/wages paid to JO employees was charged to LDRRMF for CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a.) Conduct seminars and training for the garbage collector/waste segregator and other personnel on the proper handling of solid waste disposal; and b.) Facilitate the dissemination of information and educational campaign for the awareness on proper waste handling and disposal practices towards achieving the environmental benefits that could be derived from the program.
observations.managementAction: Training for the garbage collectors were informed that there are plans to conduct garbage collector training and information dissemination, however it did not push due to COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: With several trainings not implemented due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all LGU department heads, through the GAD Focal Person to strictly observe the scheduled submission of GPB not later than March 31, a year before the budget year as provided in the JMC and to submit for DILG review and endorsement.
observations.managementAction: No copy of the approved GAD Plan and Budget, and Accomplishment Report for CY 2020 as well as the approved GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2018 were submitted to the audit team. Delay in the preparation and submission of the GPB was due to COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: Delay in the preparation and submission of GPB due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC to submit necessary documents for the computation of the remaining useful value of the purchased second-hand/ reconditioned equipment. Likewise, ensure that procurement is supported with relevant documents before any payments are made and strictly adhere to the “Guidelines on the Acquisition and Rental of Motor Vehicles” of the National Budget Circular (NBC) No. 446-A dated January 30, 1998.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has submitted documents for the computation of the remaining useful value of the purchased second-hand/reconditioned firefighting equipment. The purchase order and original purchase receipt were not available.
observations.reason: No copy of the official purchase receipt from the original purchase. Delay in the process of registration/titling due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management monitor the implementation of the programs/projects and strictly follow the guidelines set in the Bureau of Fisheries & Aquatic Resources memorandum and AO No. 10 to provide optimum realization of socio-economic growth in the locality and improvement of quality lives of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality reported that the Fisheries & Aquatic Resources were informed on the status and that some items were distributed. Delay in distribution was due to procurement and coordination issues.
observations.reason: Delay in implementation and distribution; some documentation not furnished to audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all parcels of land owned by the Municipality should be registered under the name of the Municipality for its protection from third party claims. Titling of the lots should immediately be done by concerned municipal officials.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted documents and is in the process of land titling.
observations.reason: Titling of the lots not yet completed; delay due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adhere strictly to existing LTO rules and regulations on the renewing/registration of motor vehicles. The Municipal Treasurer or Property Officer should facilitate the renewal of registration at least a month prior to its expiration to avoid penalties/surcharges on late registrations. We also recommend management prepare an inventory report of all insurable properties of the LGU and submit the same to the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS for processing of the insurance coverage of all its insurable properties.
observations.managementAction: The LGU reported that registrations and insurance processing were initiated; some vehicles were still on process of registration and insurance due to pandemic constraints.
observations.reason: Delay in registration and insurance processing due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management conduct physical count of supplies and PPE, maintain PPE ledger cards and stock/property cards, and submit the Report on Physical Count of Inventories and Report on Physical Count of PPE as required by COA Circulars.
observations.managementAction: Supplies/PPE ledger cards were prepared and submitted to the audit team where possible. Inventory Committee was not able to conduct physical inventory of PPEs of the LGUs due to COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: Inventory committee was not able to conduct physical inventory of PPEs due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management stop the grant of death benefits and burial assistance to its officers and employees without legal or statutory basis. We further recommend refund of the total amount of P116,754.40 for said payment.
observations.managementAction: COE issued by the Regional Director, COA RO8. The disallowance is on appeal.
observations.reason: Disallowance is on appeal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality should stop payments for legal services without the supporting documents required and suspend the payment until conformity or acquiescence from the Solicitor General and concurrence of COA is received. Refund should be made if necessary.
observations.managementAction: COE issued by the Regional Director, COA RO8. The employee concerned is not anymore connected with the LGU, thus there is difficulty on the part of the LGU to require for the refund.
observations.reason: No refund yet was made for the payment of attorney’s fee.

observations.recommendation:
1.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw a Journal
Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify completed Infrastructure Projects
from CIP to the appropriate Infrastructure Asset account.
1.7 Further, we recommend coordination between the Municipal
Engineering Office and Municipal Accounting Office on the
monitoring of project status to timely make appropriate
reclassification of completed projects so as not to accumulate the
amounts in the CIP account.

2.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer should ensure transfer of all current year unexpended QRF
and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under Trust
Liabilities-DRRM account.
2.7 We further recommended that reconciliation of the records of the
Municipal Budget Office, Municipal Accounting Office and the Local
Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer in as far as the
utilization of the LDRRM Fund is concerned should be done
periodically so that proper adjustment/s if any on the reports could be
made on time and under and over transfer to the Trust Liability
account could be avoided.
2.8 Also, we recommended to transfer the amount of ₱248,548.00 to Trust
Liabilities-DRRM account immediately.

3.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant:
a. To properly classify the expenditures before preparing a journal
entry voucher and record the same in the appropriate expense
account;
b. Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to reclassify the
amount of ₱856,165.00 in the Extraordinary and Miscellaneous
account and record the ₱763,505.00 as Representation Expense,
₱77,000.00 as Honoraria and ₱15,660.00 as Office Supplies Expense.
3.11 The Municipal Accountant admitted that there has been a substantial increase
in the Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expense account, and committed to
comply with the audit recommendation.

4.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer/Assistant Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant to:
a) Require the Revenue Collecting Clerks to remit the collection had
by them on a daily basis and to prepare and submit the
corresponding report of collections and deposits thereon.
b) Conduct periodic inventory of accountable forms reported on hand
by the RCC on the report of accountability of accountable forms.
c) The monthly RAAF should be reconciled with the actual balance on
hand in order to have an accurate report of the accountable forms
unissued for the period covered by the report.
d) Observe the prescribed period in the remittance of the collections
as provided in Sec 29 of the NGAS Manual in order to avoid
accumulation of cash collections on the hands of the AOs.

6.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Require the Inventory Committee to prepare a methodical and
well-planned physical inventory taking, which could be done in a
year-round basis, until all the Property, Plant and Equipment of
the Municipality have been accounted for, and the corresponding
Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE), thereon had been prepared;
b.) Direct the Property and Accounting Unit to undertake
collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in the
RPCPPE are accompanied with their corresponding correct
valuation, and details of the particular PPE; and
c.) Direct the Disposal Committee to expedite the process of the
disposal of unserviceable properties of the LGU.

7.11 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct:
a. The Municipal Agriculturist to conduct an inventory of the
Biological Assets, and to prepare an inventory report thereon
showing the fair value of each specie and submit the same to the
Municipal Accountant for proper recording and recognition of
the Biological Assets in the books;
b. The Municipal Agriculturist and the Veterinarian to prepare and
submit a necroscopy report for every death of livestock owned by
the LGU and submit the same to COA for verification;
c. The Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for
biological assets, update the same based on the report of the
Municipal Agriculturist and ensure that proper accounting and
48
recognition is made to the Financial Statements as well as to the
Notes to the Financial Statements.
d. Moreover, strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth under
IPSAS 27 in the recognition, measurement, and disclosure of
Biological Assets.

8.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local
Cooperative Development Officer to implement immediately the
DOLE Livelihood Project amounting to ₱2,520,000.00.
8.12 Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer determine the availability of cash of all fund transfers
which are reported in the books and issue the check in favor of the
Source Agencies to return all unutilized/unused amount. In case the
source agency can no longer be identified, the check shall be made in
favor of the Bureau of Treasury.

13.8 We recommended that the MDRRMO provide a separate caption for
the current and continuing appropriation on the LDRRM
Investment Plan as prescribed in COA Circular 2012-002.
13.9 Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE)
ensure that the LDRRMF is utilized solely for disaster risk
management activities.
13.10 Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer
maintain separate registries (RAAOs) for LDRRMF to facilitate
monitoring of the balances of the fund.

14.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should ensure that the
development projects be optimally utilized for the attainment of
desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the LGU.
14.12 Further, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer, General
Services Officer, and all other offices concerned should implement
92
the PPAs within the schedule of implementation as indicated in the
Annual Investment Plan and without further delay to achieve
optimal benefits from the fund.
14.13 Moreover, we recommended that funds for PPAs which are not
procurement-and-implementation-ready be realigned to other
priority PPAs which could be readily procured or implemented.

16.9 We recommended that reconciliation of the records of the Municipal
Budget Office, Municipal Accounting Office and the GAD Focal
Person, in the utilization of the GAD fund, should be done
periodically so that disbursements is within the attributed amount.
16.10 Also, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the
Department Heads to:
a) Strictly comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-
NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 on
prioritization, and training of personnel responsible for
planning and implementation of GAD programs, projects and
activities to ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-
related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are
assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that
may be provided; and
b) Observe the scheduled GAD Planning and Budgeting as
provided by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 dated
January 12, 2016 to facilitate timely submission to the
Provincial Planning and Development Office and the
subsequent review and endorsement by the DILG Provincial
Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take into consideration the recognition principle in the recording the expenditures and adhere to the rules set forth in IPSAS 17- Property, Plant and Equipment. Likewise, we recommended that the necessary adjusting entries be prepared to correct the balances of the expense and the asset accounts to fairly present the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive stop the practice of granting of cash advances for the purpose other than for the payment of salaries and wages, commutable allowances, honoraria and other benefits and petty operating expenses which cannot be paid by check. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned to prepare and submit the approved As-Built-Plan. We also recommend that henceforth the approved as-built-plan be attached to the claim prior to final payment and acceptance of the completed project.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the contractors concerned to post the subject warranty securities. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension or Disallowance in case of failure of the contractors to post the required warranty securities. Henceforth, the Municipality should strictly enforce compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR in the implementation of infrastructure projects, particularly on the posting of warranty securities before final acceptance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Finance Committee (LFC) to submit monthly the Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project/Activity to the audit team. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a separate Subsidiary Ledger for the BGCM account for easy monitoring/tracking of its utilization.

We recommended that the: a) MSWDO cause the immediate submission of the required documents as prescribed under the rules and procedures of DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 04 to ensure that the beneficiary of the social amelioration provisions comply with the documentary requirements and guarantee attainment of the primary purpose of the social protection program; and b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that the liquidation documents are complete and sufficient. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance.

We reiterate the previous year’s recommendation that Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to propose programs that will address the four thematic areas of LDRRM pursuant to the Joint Memorandum Circular. Likewise, instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to charge the personal services to the General Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) and Municipal Accountant to enforce the mandatory deduction of the employee’s share in the preparation of the payroll and remit the same including the government share to GSIS, HDMF and PHIC.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to fully remit the taxes to the Bureau of Internal Revenue immediately to avoid exposing the withholding agents of the LGU to the risk of incurring penalties and prevent the possibility of using funds for other purposes.
observations.managementAction: Payments made to suppliers on the last day of the month , thereby depriving the withholding agents of the LGU to the risk of incurring penalties. Also, the following month's payments made will be included in the following month's remittance list.
observations.reason: Payments made on the last day of the month, remitted in the following month

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive take into consideration the current circumstances and determine whether the continued hiring of these employees is necessary on proper consideration of the current and Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses and determine whether continued hiring of coaches/trainers supports the primary objective of the government to prevent, mitigate and quickly respond to the effects of the COVID 19 pandemic. Moreover, ensure that disbursements are properly supported with documents to warrant its validity. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance.
observations.reason: Some claims for honorarium still not supported with document.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Accountant review the computation of the depreciation expense and observe the established rules provided in IPSAS 17-Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE), and COA Circular No. 2003-007. We also recommended that the necessary adjusting entry be prepared to rectify the error committed.
observations.managementAction: The Mun. Accountant started the process of adjusted the computation of PPE account with negative depreciation balances. The LGU is already reviewing the computation of the depreciation with negative balances.
observations.reason: The LGU is in the process of reviewing the computation of PPE account the negative depreciation expenses.

observations.recommendation: In order to avoid the above legal remedies, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the persons liable/ responsible to immediately settle their disallowances or the Municipal Accountant to withhold any amount due them until the disallowance is fully settled. Also, it is recommended to settle the audit suspensions otherwise it will mature to an audit disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Settled this period. Notice of Settlement of Suspensions/Disallowances were issued - some installments were made because the net take home pay of the employees concerned were not enough to pay in full their disallowance.
observations.reason: With several settlements made; remaining balances settled by installment due to insufficient net take-home pay.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) in the preparation of the payroll to enforce the mandatory deduction of the employee’s share and remit the same including the government share to GSIS, HDMF and PHIC.
observations.reason: Mandatory deduction will start on March, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 1. Instruct the HRMO to follow the rules and regulation in the grant of monetization of leave credits; 2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to verify and process claims for payment of monetization of leave credits supported with complete documentation; 3. Require the persons concerned to submit the required justification of the claims. Failure would result to suspension in audit of the transaction; and 4. Require the persons liable to refund the amount in excess of the allowed claim. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality stopped the granting of monetization of leave credits in excess of the allowed minimum of 10 days to a maximum of 30 days.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer immediately remit the taxes withheld from payments for supplies/materials and services of the Municipality. observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer remitted taxes to BIR; however the remaining balance was remitted after the preparation of the remittance list.
observations.reason: Taxes remitted to BIR; remaining balances included in subsequent remittance list.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to expedite the implementation of programs/projects and return the unutilized balance of completed projects to the source agencies. Also submit copies of the Memorandum of Agreements (MOAs) for each fund received that serve as a guide in the implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: The Management returned the unutilized balance for completed projects to source agencies and submitted MOAs where applicable.
observations.reason: Some projects completed and returned to source agencies; others still in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the grant of PEI in excess of the authorized amount and require the recipient officials and employees to refund the amount in excess of the authorized amount as prescribed in EO No. 80 and DBM BC No. 2014-3. We also recommended that the disallowance be settled until refunded; otherwise Notices of Suspension or Disallowance will be issued.
observations.managementAction: Notice of Settlement of Suspensions/Disallowances were issued and some installments were made. COE issued because the net take home pay of the employees concerned are not enough to pay in full the disallowance.
observations.reason: Partial settlement; some disallowances under appeal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management stop the grant of allowances in the absence of any legal or statutory basis and require the persons liable to refund the amounts received for unauthorized calamity financial assistance and communication allowance. Henceforth stop the grant of allowances without statutory basis.
observations.managementAction: COE issued by the Regional Director, COA RO8. The disallowance is on appeal.
observations.reason: Some disallowances were subject of appeal.

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare a journal entry voucher to record the
disallowances which had become final and executory having been issued
with NFD and COE as Receivables. Also, we recommend that the
Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for each
official/employee determined liable/responsible for the amount
disallowed for monitoring of the liability and settlement.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to review the computation of the depreciation expense and
observe the established rules provided in IPSAS 17-Property, Plant and
Equipment (PPE), and COA Circular No. 2003-007. We also recommend
that the necessary adjusting entry be prepared to rectify the error
committed.

We recommended the following courses of action:
The Municipal Engineering Office:
 Provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Services
Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road
component for road projects; as well as the report on the
repair/maintenance of each local road network;
The General Services Officer/Inventory Committee:
 Prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Local
Road Network (RPCLRN);
 Maintain the Local Road Network Property Cards for each roads
and road components;
The Municipal Accountant
 Adhere to the accounting policy on non-inclusion of residual value
in the computation of the depreciation expense for the depreciable
component of the Local Road Network;
 Prepare the adjusting entry to correct the understatement of
Depreciation Expense to correct the error committed;
 Maintain subsidiary records for road and road components for
every road network.

We recommended to the Management to immediately implement
poverty reduction programs/projects/activities in order to reach
economic growth and not to deprive constituents of the benefits that may
derive therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipality fully utilize the 20% DF for
priority development projects in order to achieve the objectives as
envisioned in DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated
February 22, 2017. We recommended further that the LGU refrain from
utilizing the 20% DF for administrative expenses or any expenses that do
not partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive:
1.) To utilize the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Funds that shall address the four thematic areas of Disaster
Prevention, Disaster Mitigation, Disaster Preparedness, Disaster
Response and Disaster Rehabilitation Recovery. Please refer to the
NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Memorandum No. 2013-1
dated March 25, 2013 for the allowable projects and activities.
2.) To instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Officer to
propose and recommend programs to be included in the AIP that
are allowed under the Circular.
3.) To instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant
to charge to the Fund only disaster related expenditures. While the
maintenance and other operating expenditures, and capital outlay of
the LDRRM Office should be charged to the General Fund.
4.) The Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry
to correct the erroneous classification of the motorcycle as Motor
Vehicle instead of Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment in the
financial statements.
5.) To stop incurring unnecessary expenditures.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
a.) Conduct seminars and training for the garbage collector/waste
segregator and other personnel on the proper handling of solid
waste disposal; and
b.) Facilitate the dissemination of information and educational
campaign for the awareness on proper waste handling and disposal
practices towards achieving the environmental benefits that could
be derived from the program.

To correct the deficiencies, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor
and the HRMO:
1. To determine the necessity, assess the duties and functions, taking
into account their expertise, experience and capacity to handle the
position in order to come up with the ideal number of personnel to
be hired;
2. To provide in the contract of service the specific details or
information such as expected service to be rendered, funding
charges, daily rate and to have it signed by the JO employee as
acknowledgement to the agreement;
3. Require each JO worker to prepare the accomplishment report
duly verified and accepted by the immediate supervisor;
4. Observe proper documentation, sound management and fiscal
administration in order to attain efficiency in the use of
government funds.

In order to avoid the above legal remedies, we recommended that the
Municipal Mayor require the Persons Liable/Responsible to immediately
settle their disallowances or require the Municipal Accountant to
withhold any amount due them until the disallowance is fully settled.

Also, it is recommended to settle the audit suspensions otherwise it will
mature to an audit disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human
Resource Management Officer (HRMO) in the preparation of the payroll

to enforce the mandatory deduction of the employee’s share and remit
the same including the government share to GSIS, HDMF and PHIC.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs all LGU department
heads, through the GAD Focal Person to strictly observe the scheduled
submission of GPD not later than March 31, a year before the budget
year as provided in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 dated
January 12, 2016 to facilitate timely submission to the Provincial
Planning and Development Office and the subsequent review and
endorsement by the DILG Provincial Office.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:
1. Instruct the HRMO to follow rules and regulation in the grant of
monetization of leave credits;
2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to verify and process claims for
payment of monetization supported with complete documentation;
3. Require the employees concerned to submit the required
justification of the claims. Failure would result to suspension in
audit of the transaction;

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the reciprocal
accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and effect the
appropriate adjustments to correct the account balances.

We recommend that management strictly observe DOH Administrative
Order No. 163, series of 2002 in all requisitions and procurements of drugs
and medicines. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant strictly
observe PPSAS 12 in accounting for all inventories.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer submit
immediately the required accounts. Henceforth, strictly observe the timely
submission of accounts and reports in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160
and Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006. This recommendation is
without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance on the
unsubmitted accounts and/or results of audit of the covering transactions.

We recommend that management (a) enforce liquidation of the funds
received from the National Government Agencies through submission of
liquidation reports in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013; (b) fast track
implementation of the project since funds are readily available in order not to
deprive the intended beneficiaries of its immediate use; and (c) return to the
Source Agency the balance of unutilized fund for projects already completed to
enable the national government to reprogram the funds for other felt-need
programs/projects.

We recommend that management ensure that the budgetary requirement
for the 20% development fund be strictly appropriated and utilized for plans/
programs/projects included in its Annual Investment Plan (AIP) in
accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and DILG-DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1. Further, to achieve desirable socio-
economic development and environmental management outcomes we also
recommend the immediate implementation of the projects within the budget
year.

We recommend that management strictly observe the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2012-002 in order to attain an effective utilization, monitoring
and control of the LDRRMF for the benefit of intended beneficiaries. Likewise,
we require the submission of the necessary documents in the payment for job
orders, meals and snacks and hauling of GI sheets from DSWD.

We recommend that the Solid Waste Management Board prepare its 10-
year Solid Waste Management Plan compliant with the requirements and
provisions set forth in RA 9003, and implement the plan to ensure efficient
disposal of Municipality’s solid wastes. Likewise, require the Municipal Health
Officer to provide the collectors with necessary trainings on the proper handling
and disposal of wastes.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Award
Committee (BAC) to submit to the audit team copies of all contracts and
purchase orders within five (5) days after its perfection/approval. Likewise, we
require the complete submission of necessary procurement documents outlined
in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of R.A. 9184 and
COA Circular No. 2012-001. This recommendation is without prejudice to the
result of post-audit.

We recommend that management refrain from procurement through
reimbursement or cash advance where direct payment to suppliers/
contractors is possible and strictly implement the provisions of COA
Circular No. 97-002 and the Government Procurement Reform Act (RA
9184).

We recommend that management submit the lacking documents required
in Section 13.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 and register the Deed of Sale on the
subject lot with the Land Registration Authority in order to facilitate the
issuance of a certificate of title in favor of the Municipality.

We recommend that management require the Philippine Councilor’s
League, Vice Mayor’s League of the Philippines and League of Municipalities
of the Philippines organized by elected officials, to issue a government official
receipt (Accountable Form No. 51) for all collections made so that proper
financial accountability could be established. Further, ensure that all future
payment to these leagues for membership dues, registration fees and others be
receipted with a government official receipt which is AF No. 51.

We recommend that management adhere strictly to existing LTO rules
and regulations on the registration/renewal of motor vehicles. The Municipal
Treasurer or Property Officer should facilitate the renewal of registration at
least a month prior to its expiration to avoid penalties/surcharges on late
registrations. We also recommend that management prepare an inventory
report of all insurable properties of the LGU and submit the same to the General
Insurance Fund of the GSIS for processing of the insurance coverage of all its
insurable properties.

We recommend that management exercise prudence in spending
government funds. Likewise, observe austerity measures in the holding of
Christmas parties, founding anniversaries, fiestas or any other occasions and
financial transaction of the Agency as prescribed under Administrative Order
No. 103.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to prepare the necessary adjusting entry for the stale checks so that the Cash in Bank and Payable accounts are fairly presented in the financial statements. Likewise, submit the required paid checks and debit/credit memos along with the bank reconciliation statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR were adjusted to cash-in-Bank and credited to Accounts payable, while, paid checks were already submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management conduct physical inventory of Supplies and Property, Plant and Equipment and render a report thereon duly categorized by type of the supplies and property, plant and equipment. We also recommend that the required Supplies/PPE Ledger Cards of the accounting unit and Stock/Property Cards of the property unit, be properly and completely maintained for purposes of reconciliation pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 92-386 and Section 111 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR Stock/Property Cards were prepared.
observations.reason: No inventory of properties was conducted for CY 2016 and the property unit still failed to prepare Stock/Property Cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to draw a journal entry voucher to reclassify the amount to the appropriate account Due from NGAs and require the agency concerned to submit immediately the liquidation report to the Accountant. Moreover, the LGU should submit the required lacking documents, and henceforth strictly observe COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, on the proper documentation of all fund transfers.
observations.managementAction: Requested liquidation of said financial assistance claimant submitted a liquidation report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer and all accountable officers to strictly observe Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 on the submission of accounts and financial reports. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of the corresponding Notice of Suspension/Disallowance on the unsubmitted accounts and/or results of post-audit of transactions.
observations.managementAction: Required DVs, payrolls and cancelled checks were now included in the Report of Accountable Forms.
observations.reason: Submission of accounts was still delayed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop the grant of additional cash advances to officials and employees with previous balances and require immediate liquidation/settlement of their unliquidated cash advances through the issuance of demand letters copy furnished the Audit Team. In case of non-compliance, instruct the Accounting Office to withhold the salaries or any money due them as provided in Item 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Management issued demand letters and copy furnished the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Outstanding unliquidated advances remained; unliquidated Advances as of December 31, 2016 totaled P31,747.14.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and all accountable officers to comply with the requirement concerning the reporting, verification, recording, deposits and remittance of collections, and also the inclusion of checks in the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms. Henceforth, refrain from involving job orders in the collecting activities.
observations.managementAction: Daily collections were recorded and checks were included in the Report of Accountable Forms. Job orders were refrained from collecting activities.
observations.reason: Daily collections by collecting officers were still not remitted every day.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002, COA Circular No. 2014-002 and COA Circular No. 97-002. Henceforth, discontinue the procurement through reimbursement and cash advance where direct payment is possible, and require the submission of the necessary supporting documents for purchases and donations.

observations.reason: Documents relative to receipt/issuance and procurement through reimbursement/cash advance for relief goods in CY 2015 were still not submitted.
